stem: An engineer takes 30 measurements of room temperature using a thermometer. The measurements have a mean of 125.5 °C and a standard deviation of 13.4 °C. However, calibration tests reveal that a thermometer systematically overestimates the true value by between 4.3% and 9.6%. Estimate the true room temperature and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 125.5 °C. After correcting for the 6.9% bias: ≈ 117.4 °C. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.3%-9.6%) and sampling variability: [109.6, 125.2]. Point estimate: 117.4 °C.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
